ab: Iha’an Ququaz ca lumaq naam tu takiucang, masipulin a mulusqu munha’an Qabinaz, ua’ takaha’anin bukzav miqumic.
ch: 小時候我們家住在曲冰，上學以後，就搬到了埔里，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Qabinaz. After I started school, we moved to Puli and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: Masipulin is sipulandaing, masipulak is itu iniqumicas Bununcuku’ a sipulun, minqanciap tu maca Bununcuku’ ha’an Taivang ti’ a madia’ sala ca vaivivaivi ka iniqumican.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: Daqat, iha’an haip qamisan tu tanabal dii, muskun azak it tuqasnanaz mun’acang macicingav is madadaingaz, asat maqanciap is inak’anak a iniqumican.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: Punmavaau’unin qamisan ca madaingaz dii, uutu maupang is talnaqtungin a bunun saduu’an.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: Pacingvan zaku ka cia tu ma’az ca qalinga qancaipun bazbaz, mantan a cia maqancia bazbaz is malasqai’ial, maqanciap amin bazbaz is malastauluu ciin malaspuut.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的日本話說得很流利，也會說國語和閩南話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Japanese, as well as Atayal, Hokkien and Chinese.

ab: Macingavak cia ha maqanciapin tu, tauluu ca binanau’az cia, madia’ sala ca puut pacqalan ha’an kukuza’an cia, daqat madia’ ca qalingaz qancaipun cia bazbaz.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是漢人，而他工作上認識許多閩南人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Chinese, and he knows many Hokkien at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: Uutu pacingavan zami tudiip a, palaqanciap ca madaingaz diis madia’ sala ka itu qabas palaciat qanup a taiklac.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: Altupat nii’ tu aminun macqal tu tukukua ca sinbabazbaz, Uutu kuci’aun zami tingvatikic pinqaningu, na maqtu’ang sicqasun tan’a.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: Uutu takiucang azak nii’ang macqal is inak’anak a iniqumican, maz’av tukuc dii bununcuku’ ca siduq inaak amin.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: Tudiipin tu minmadia’in ca sinpacqalan, mina’is’ang maqanciap tu maca inakan inaak Bununi’ a mantan makanvaivi, maqtu’ang iskukuniv.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為布農族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Bunun is unique and something to be proud of.
